notes.note: Arpinum was the Volscian hill-town in southern Latium where Cicero (and Marius before him) was born; it had held full Roman citizenship since 188 BC. As a patron of his native municipality Cicero routinely lent it the weight of his name.

Cisalpine Gaul, the province north of the Apennines, governed by M. Iunius Brutus in this period; Arpinum's revenue-bearing lands lay within it, which is why a Roman commendation to its governor mattered for the town's finances.

Equites Romani, members of the equestrian order, the propertied class ranking just below the senators; most provincial and municipal financial business was carried by men of this status. The three named men are sent as the municipality's legati (agents).
At Arpinum the only elected magistracy was a college of three aediles. The year Cicero is writing of, the three slots were filled by his son Marcus, his nephew Quintus, and his friend M. Caesius.
